Off Calendar Apr-04-2024 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT .
Off Calendar Feb-27-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Andrew C. McClelland; Lunski & Stimpert LLP; 415-426-3586; appearing as counsel for defendants/cross-complainants California Gilrl LLC, et al.
Joseph K. Bravo; Bravo Law Offices; 415-512-6700; appearing as counsel for plaintiff James Bong Enterprises, Inc.

Mr. McClelland contests the court's tentative decision. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden and show that the entire claim for $46,400 is subject to res judicata/collateral estoppel due to small claims judgment. No evidence is provided to show what this amount is comprised of (the amounts claimed for rent; the amounts claimed for parking; the amounts claimed for late fees), and if each element of this claim has been addressed in the small claims action.
For example, moving party appears to suggest (no declaration or discovery responses provided to this effect) that $46,440 includes, inter alia, unspecified amounts due for parking fees. While landlord's complaint references parking, there is no allegation as to the nature of the contract for parking and whether it was a lease that was due to expire concurrently with the Office Lease attached to the Complaint. Tenant states that there was "an oral agreement to rent a parking spot for $300 per month." No further terms of this oral agreement are unspecified. (Baker Declaration, paragraph 7). The small claims judgment does not reference any oral agreements or parking; it relieves the tenant of the balance of the lease. The only lease with a balance presented to the Court is the Office Lease. There is no evidence that there was (a) a lease between the parties for parking, that (2) was addressed in the small claims action or (3) of which the tenant was relieved via small claims judgment.
If tenant's assumption is correct and the landlord's claim includes parking fees post December 2020, it is impossible to determine whether the entirety of the Plaintiff's sole cause of action for breach of contract is barred by res judicata/collateral estoppel based on the record before this Court as to the claims in this action and the issues presented/resolved in small claims.
Late-filed opposition is stricken.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-15-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Marc Stimpert, 415.426.3586 of Lunski & Stimpert LLP appearing via Zoom for Defendant Marielle Baker.
Josephy Bravo, 415.512.6700 of Bravo Law Offices appeaing via Zoom for Plaintiff James Bong Enterprises, Inc.

Hearing held. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT MARIELLEN BAKER, CALIFORNIA GIRL, LLC, CALIFORNIA GIRL JEWELRY DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer by Ms. Baker, an individual, is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts in support of each element of the cause of action for breach of contract as to this defendant. Demurrer is otherwise overruled. Based on the allegations in the complaint and the judgment in the small claims action it is impossible to ascertain at the pleadings stage if the breach of lease from January 2021 was fully adjudicated in the small claims action re: $46,440 sought in this action. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)
